summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #297, #298, and #299 for the ars-ui Rust component library: add a shared ColorValue foundation and 8 framework-agnostic color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) as a single combined PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented with full test coverage (>90% line and branch), pushed to PR #698 with all 22 CI checks passing after a coverage fix commit. Three rounds of Codex review completed, addressing ~20 findings across correctness, accessibility, and robustness. PR awaits Codex 👍 before merge.

repo: core_maths::CoreFloat is the workspace's no_std float convention; use fully-qualified form CoreFloat::method(x). It lacks min/max — need private fmin/fmax helpers.
MessageFn From<F> impls must be hand-written per unique function signature; there is no blanket impl for arbitrary closures.

Slider drag pattern: adapter drives DragMove/DragEnd via its own pointer listeners; core fires typed Effect::ChangeEnd on DragEnd to invoke on_change_end. spec's use_platform_effects() and track_pointer_drag() do not exist in ars-core.
AngleSlider's Part::Marker { value: f64 } cannot use #[derive(ComponentPart)] because f64 is not Eq/Hash; requires a manual impl using to_bits().
Bindable::get() returns the controlled prop in controlled mode; set() updates internal. New Bindable::pending() accessor was added to read the just-dragged value before the parent syncs.
CssProperty::Custom renders as --{name}, so the name argument must not include the -- prefix.

cargo xci-fast runs 10 gates: fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, snapshot-count. snapshot-count requires ≥1 insta snapshot per state variant.
CI branch coverage is measured via cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace (default features), then check-all enforces a 90% branch / 90% line threshold for ars-components.
trim_start_matches('#') strips ALL leading # chars; use strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) to strip at most one.
Workspace uses unpinned stable toolchain; stable 1.96 introduced unnecessary_option_map_or_else lint which broke pre-existing pagination code under the workspace-wide clippy::suspicious = deny rule.
Codex bot is chatgpt-codex-connector[bot] and can hit rate limits ('reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews') — not a code problem, requires quota reset.

Machine on_props_changed + SyncValue/SetProps event pattern (mirroring input/slider) is required for controlled-mode prop updates to apply after mount; without it, value/disabled/readonly go stale until remount.
The disabled guard in drag machines must pass DragEnd through unconditionally; blocking it on disable wedges the machine in Dragging state with no recovery path.
color_wheel Increment/Decrement used % (which keeps negative remainders) for hue wrap; must use rem_euclid(360.0).
Hue channel in a slider requires a separate unwrapped slider_value field in Context because ColorValue::new wraps hue into [0,360), causing SetToMax at 360° to jump the thumb back to 0.

code.finding: from_hex used trim_start_matches('#') (strips all leading #) and then byte-sliced without ASCII check — '##3366ff' was accepted and non-ASCII (e.g. #ああ) panicked at a non-char boundary. Fix: strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) + is_ascii() guard.

parse_rgb_args / parse_hsl_args / parse_hsb_args used parse::<f64>() which succeeds for NaN/inf; ColorValue::new only debug_asserts finiteness, so release mode stored NaN in styles/ARIA. Fixed with parse_finite_f64 helper.

Needed 4 new From<F> impls for color signatures: Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), and two valuetext arities. Must be added here for each unique closure signature used in component Messages structs.

Added Bindable::pending() returning &T (the internal/drag value) alongside the existing get() which returns the controlled prop. Required because drag change-end callbacks were reading get() and reporting the stale controlled value in controlled mode.

format_value showed saturation/lightness as percentages (e.g. 50) but commit_input parsed '50' and clamped to [0,1] -> 1.0, silently corrupting the color. Fix: format_value multiplies by 100, commit_input divides by 100 before channel_range clamp.

SetProps updated channel/color_format but left ctx.input_text in the old representation; the field showed the wrong format until the user next focused it. Fix: reformat input_text from current value with new channel/format when not focused.
Props::readonly set aria-readonly but not native HTML readonly and the machine accepted Change/blur commits while readonly. Fix: set HtmlAttr::ReadOnly and guard Change/blur in transition.
has_description flag had no setter event, making the entire aria-describedby Description-part branch permanently dead code. Added Event::SetHasDescription(bool) mirroring the field component pattern.

In idle state, focused_index is None so roving tabindex gave every item tabindex=-1, making the widget unreachable by keyboard Tab. Fix: tabbable_index() returns selected-or-first item as tabindex=0 in idle.
Thumb set aria-labelledby to a *-label id but ColorWheel has no Label part, so the IDREF dangled. Drop aria-labelledby; aria-label is already present on the thumb.
Increment/Decrement used % for hue wrap which produces negative remainders (e.g. PageDown large_step=720 from hue=10 stored negative hue). Fix: rem_euclid(360.0).

Hue slider max (360°) wrapped to 0° via ColorValue::new's [0,360) invariant, causing the thumb to jump. Fix: added slider_value: f64 field in Context that tracks the unwrapped channel value; thumb/aria-valuenow/position read it; color derives from it.
SetProps updated min/max but left ctx.value out-of-range; aria-valuenow and hidden input reflected the stale out-of-range value. Fix: re-clamp ctx.value to new [min, max] bounds in SetProps.

workflow: waiting-for-codex-review skill: post @codex review, start a background poll watching for 👀 dropped + unresolved threads or 👍, fix each finding TDD (failing test first), push, reply+resolve inline, re-trigger. Repeat until 👍.

For branch coverage gaps, reproduce CI's exact measurement locally: cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace --lcov then cargo xtask coverage check-all. The --workspace flag includes cross-crate integration tests that add ~1.1% over -p-only measurement.
One-armed if flag { ... } conditionals require explicit both-arms test coverage (idle+active/enabled+disabled) to satisfy branch thresholds. Add dense coverage tests to each stateful module.

Spec drift should be reconciled in the same PR as the implementation; use a spec-sync agent to mirror contract changes (new events, type signatures, API changes) across all affected spec markdown files after the code stabilizes.
Background watcher scripts must avoid the variable name 'status' in zsh — it is read-only and causes immediate exit code 1. Use cov_state or similar.
xci-fast snapshot-count gate fails if any state variant lacks at least one insta snapshot. Add separate snapshot tests for each machine state.
Pre-existing clippy lints from a new stable toolchain version can block an unrelated PR; investigate whether the lint is from your code or pre-existing before deciding to fix vs. note.

friction: Codex was rate-limited ('You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews') on both initial review triggers, blocking the review loop until quota reset.
Extensive spec drift: specs referenced non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects(), track_pointer_drag(), generate_id()) requiring all 8 spec files to be reconciled after implementation.
core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max methods, requiring private fmin/fmax helpers despite core_maths being the established no_std float dep.
Stable 1.96 introduced unnecessary_option_map_or_else lint that fired on pre-existing pagination code under the workspace-wide clippy::suspicious = deny, blocking CI for all PRs.
Coverage CI failure after initial push: branch coverage landed at 89.7% (threshold 90%) due to one-armed if flag {} conditionals in connect methods, requiring a second commit.
Background watch script crashed twice due to zsh read-only 'status' variable; required renaming to a different variable name.
Three full rounds of Codex review (~20 findings total across passes), many of which were cross-cutting and required the same fix pattern across 4-6 files simultaneously.
spec-compile-snippets gate compiles spec code blocks in isolation, requiring spec reconciliation to be done carefully to not introduce new compile errors in spec blocks.
MessageFn requires a new From<F> impl for each unique closure signature; discovering this required reading source code since there is no compile-time hint pointing to message_fn.rs.
open_items: Codex 👍 still pending — PR #698 cannot merge until Codex approves. Re-trigger @codex review once the chatgpt-codex-connector bot quota resets.

ColorValue could be promoted to spec/shared/color-types.md (currently defined inline in color-picker.md §Types); deferred as orthogonal to implementation correctness — touches manifest + 8 spec frontmatters.
Adapter specs for all 8 color components (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*.md) describe future framework-specific adapter implementations — explicitly out of scope for this PR.

Foundation spec (spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md) MessageFn From-impl listing is a representative sample and was not exhaustively updated to include all color-component signatures; intentionally left inconsistent rather than partially updated.
block v0.1.6 transitive-dep future-incompat note in workspace clippy output — pre-existing, unrelated to color work, needs a separate dep upgrade.

The color_area F6 value_text message signature changed from Fn(f64,f64,&str,&str,&Locale) to Fn(&str,&str,&str,&Locale) — any existing adapter code consuming this message will need updating when adapters are implemented.
